Upgrade the installed Laurel CLI when the install method supports it

Usage:
  laurel upgrade [--dry-run] [--json]

Options:
  --dry-run           Print the detected upgrade command without running it
  -j, --json          Emit the upgrade plan or result as JSON
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --dry-run → LAUREL_UPGRADE_DRY_RUN

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel upgrade
  laurel upgrade --dry-run
  LAUREL_NO_UPDATE_CHECK=1 laurel upgrade       # skip self-update checks and actions
